summary: Cluster 4 focuses on Digital, Industry and Space. It addresses key areas including advanced manufacturing, circular industries, digital and emerging technologies, quantum and high-performance computing, and the EU's strategic autonomy in accessing and using space. The destinations and themes under this cluster support Europe's twin green and digital transitions while enhancing technological leadership and industrial resilience.

The theme 'Manufacturing' contains 3 calls. Key topics include: Integrated approaches for remanufacturing (Made in Europe Partnership), Physical and cognitive augmentation in advanced manufacturing (Made in Europe Partnership), Advanced manufacturing technologies for leadership of EU manufacturers in products for the netzero industry (Made in Europe Partnership).
The theme 'Construction' contains 1 calls. Key topics include: Enhanced logistics and operations of construction sites.

id: Energy-Intensive Industries - Decarbonisation and Energy Efficiency
summary: The theme 'Energy-Intensive Industries - Decarbonisation and Energy Efficiency' contains 5 calls. Key topics include: From heat-driven processes to the use of mechanical and electric forces (Processes4Planet partnership), Green and resilient flexible production processes (Processes4Planet partnership), Integrated use of renewable energy carriers in industrial sites (Processes4Planet partnership).

id: Energy-intensive Industries - Circularity and Zero Pollution
summary: The theme 'Energy-intensive Industries - Circularity and Zero Pollution' contains 4 calls. Key topics include: Developing and embedding upcycling technologies into viable business (Processes4Planet partnership), Safe and clean processing technologies and products (Processes4Planet partnership), Synergies and mutual learning with national and regional initiatives in Europe on Industrial decarbonisation.
The theme 'Social Circular Enterprises' contains 1 calls. Key topics include: Demonstrators for clusters of social circular enterprises.

Destination 1 includes 14 calls grouped under 5 themes. The destination addresses major challenges such as: Manufacturing, Construction, Energy-Intensive Industries - Decarbonisation and Energy Efficiency, Energy-intensive Industries - Circularity and Zero Pollution, Social Circular Enterprises.

The theme 'Raw Materials' contains 4 calls. Key topics include: Technologies for critical raw materials and strategic raw materials from end-of-life products, Strategic Partnerships for Raw Materials: Innovative Approaches for sustainable production of Critical Raw Materials, Innovative solutions for the sustainable production for Semiconductor raw materials.

The theme 'Innovative Advanced Materials' contains 6 calls. Key topics include: Innovative Advanced Materials (IAMs) for product monitoring, smart maintenance and repair strategies in the construction sector, Innovative Advanced Materials (IAMs) for robust, fast curing sealants and coatings for manufacturing and final assembly, Innovative Advanced Materials Innovation Procurement.

The theme 'Safe and Sustainable by Design' contains 2 calls. Key topics include: Development of safe and sustainable by design alternatives to Perfluoroalkyl and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS), Accelerate the uptake of lifecycle assessment (LCA) for Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD) chemicals and materials and resulting products.
The theme 'Textiles' contains 1 calls. Key topics include: Digitally enabled local-forlocal textile and apparel production (Textiles for the Future Partnership).
Destination 2 includes 13 calls grouped under 4 themes. The destination addresses major challenges such as: Raw Materials, Innovative Advanced Materials, Safe and Sustainable by Design, Textiles.

The theme 'Connected Collaborative Computing Networks (3C networks)' contains 5 calls. Key topics include: Large-scale pilots for supply end-to-end infrastructures integrating device, network computing and communication capabilities for Telco Edge Cloud deployments, as a basis for Connected Collaborative Computing Networks (3C networks), Alignment of stakeholders towards the supply-side large-scale pilot of end-to-end infrastructures integrating device, network computing and communication capabilities, Roadmap for next generation computing technologies from IoT device level to edge to cloud to HPC.
The theme 'AI-GenAI / Data / Robotics' contains 1 calls. Key topics include: Fostering Innovative and Compliant Data Ecosystems.
Destination 3 includes 6 calls grouped under 2 themes. The destination addresses major challenges such as: Connected Collaborative Computing Networks (3C networks), AI-GenAI / Data / Robotics.

The theme 'Quantum and High Performance Computing' contains 4 calls. Key topics include: Continuation of the Quantum Technologies Flagship, Quantum Computing – complementing the quantum computing FPAs with the development of a technology agnostic software stack, Supporting Digital Partnerships in Quantum technologies.
The theme 'Photonics' contains 1 calls. Key topics include: Advanced sensor technologies and multimodal sensor integration for multiple application domains.
The theme 'Semiconductors' contains 1 calls. Key topics include: Strengthening the fabless Startup and SME ecosystem in Europe.

The theme 'AI-GenAI / Data / Robotics' contains 5 calls. Key topics include: Robust and trustworthy GenerativeAI for Robotics and industrial automation, Challenge-Driven GenAI4EU Booster, Assessment methodologies for General Purpose AI capabilities and risks.
The theme 'Artificial Intelligence in Science' contains 2 calls. Key topics include: AI Foundation models in science (GenAI4EU), Facilitated cooperation for AI in Science.
